Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of the module's three main topic areas — environment setup, Python data types and structures, and control structures — that serves as a navigation guide for both learners and instructors to understand the scope and sequence of the module.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: An overview page listing all topics in the Python Basics for Data Analysis module, including installing Python and configuring the analytics environment, used by learners and instructors to navigate the module's contents.

Artifacts.Name: Python_Basics_for_generated.pptx
Artifacts.Description: A slide deck introducing Python basics for data analytics students, covering environment setup (installing Python, pip, and virtual environments), verification of the setup, and foundational Python concepts, intended for use as lecture material or a self-paced visual reference.

Artifacts.Name: Setting Up the Analytics Environment
Artifacts.Description: A reading that guides learners through setting up a Python development environment for data analytics, covering the Python interpreter, essential libraries, and virtual environments so they are ready to write and run analytics code.

Artifacts.Name: Python Data Types and Structures
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers Python's core built-in data types (integers, floats, strings, booleans) and data structures (lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets), explaining how each works and how to choose the right one to support accurate and efficient data cleaning and analysis tasks.

Artifacts.Name: Control Structures and Logic
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains how to use conditional statements, loops, and other control-flow mechanisms in Python to direct program execution, make decisions, and repeat operations — skills applied directly to filtering, iterating over, and processing datasets.

Artifacts.Name: Writing and Using Functions
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers how to define and call Python functions, including concepts such as parameters, return values, and reusability, so learners can organize analytical code into modular, repeatable blocks rather than duplicating logic throughout a script.

Artifacts.Name: File Input and Output for Data
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains how to read data from and write data to external files in Python — particularly text and CSV files — covering file-system interaction methods that allow learners to load real-world datasets into scripts and save processed results.

Artifacts.Name: Writing Foundational Data Analysis Scripts
Artifacts.Description: This reading shows learners how to combine Python fundamentals — data types, control structures, functions, and file I/O — into a complete, structured data analysis script that takes raw input data and produces meaningful analytical output.

Artifacts.Name: Setting Up the Analytics Environment
Artifacts.Description: A reading that guides learners through setting up a Python development environment for data analytics, covering the Python interpreter, essential libraries, and virtual environments so they are ready to write and run analytics code.

Artifacts.Name: Quiz Question Bank (20 questions)
Artifacts.Description: A 20-question bank assessing learners on Python environment setup (recommended version, pip, virtual environments), Jupyter Notebook features, and key analytics libraries such as pandas and NumPy, for use in quizzes or knowledge checks throughout the module.
